title: Italian municipality-level database of single- and multi-hazard scores
author: Tocchi, Gabriella
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The database presents the results of applying the index-based procedure in Italy to rank single and multiple natural hazards and identify multi-hazard hotspots. The approach integrates intensity-based single-hazard scores derived from open-source data, selected using consistent return periods or equivalent intensity proxies, and normalized through a uniform methodology to ensure comparability across hazards. For each Italian municipality, the database provides the single-hazard scores for six natural hazards - earthquakes, floods, landslides, tsunamis, heatwaves, and heavy rainfall - as well as composite multi-hazard scores that combine these hazards into a single, aggregated metric.
steps_raw: The database presents the results of applying the index-based procedure in Italy to rank single and multiple natural hazards and identify multi-hazard hotspots. The approach integrates intensity-based single-hazard scores derived from open-source data, selected using consistent return periods or equivalent intensity proxies, and normalized through a uniform methodology to ensure comparability across hazards. For each Italian municipality, the database provides the single-hazard scores for six natural hazards - earthquakes, floods, landslides, tsunamis, heatwaves, and heavy rainfall - as well as composite multi-hazard scores that combine these hazards into a single, aggregated metric.
